review_text: I ordered a SACHS flywheel (2294 501 267) from Autodoc, paying a premium SACHS price.What I received was a broken, recycled LUK flywheel, inside a SACHS box, tied together with zip ties and taped shut. The flywheel had huge play throughout, making it completely unsafe for installation.SACHS/ZF confirmed that this flywheel is not even produced by SACHS, but sourced from LUK for rare VWAG models. Yet Autodoc never disclosed this anywhere, selling it as SACHS at a premium price.The price difference is shocking: in Bulgaria it can be €350–€450, while on other European sites it is only about €150, yet the product inside is identical to a much cheaper LUK part. Autodoc profits massively by misleading customers.This is misleading, unsafe, and completely unacceptable. I returned the part the same day, but Autodoc failed to resolve the issue, leaving my car unusable for 2 months and causing 4 months of delays, frustration, and extra costs.Autodoc must stop misleading customers and clearly disclose when parts are sourced from third parties, especially when they are broken or unsafe.
supplier_response: Hello Kristian Karakashev! We sincerely apologize for the experience you’ve had with the flywheel. We understand your frustration regarding the incorrect part, safety concerns, and the inconvenience caused by the delays. Please be informed that our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to update with your case in detail and assist you further to resolve this matter. Your feedback will also be used to improve our product verification and disclosure processes. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Very disappointing and misleading experience with AutodocMy experience with Autodoc has been extremely frustrating and disappointing.I ordered spare parts after Autodoc’s website explicitly confirmed compatibility with my vehicle. My VIN number is already saved in my personal account, and based on this information, the website assured that the parts were suitable. However, when the parts arrived, they turned out to be completely incompatible.As a result, I had to return the items and paid shipping costs twice (to and from Geneva), totaling approximately CHF 85. Autodoc refunded only the price of the parts and refused to reimburse the shipping costs, even though the mistake was entirely theirs.Instead of taking responsibility, they attempted to blame the customer by claiming that selecting parts by make and model is “not accurate.” This raises a serious question: why does their website confirm compatibility if it is unreliable?This was not my first bad experience with Autodoc. Previously, I was offered a compensation voucher for a similar mistake, which later turned out to be invalid and unusable. Now they repeat the same approach by offering a small discount coupon instead of refunding the actual costs caused by their error.In summary:Incorrect and incompatible partsCustomer forced to pay for the company’s mistakesUnreliable vouchersAvoidance instead of real solutionsA very poor customer experience. I will not use Autodoc again and cannot recommend them.
supplier_response: Hello Sabah Jassem! We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and frustration you’ve experienced. Please be aware that the parts displayed on our website whether selected through the vehicle selector or by entering your registration number are intended as general recommendations. Because vehicles often have unique specifications beyond just the model and body type, the most accurate way to confirm compatibility is by using the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or the Original Equipment Number (OEN). Even if your VIN is stored in your account, our system does not automatically verify compatibility against it. This important step must be carried out by our trained specialists to ensure the correct fit. Our customer support team is always available to help with a full compatibility check, as product listings only provide technical details and cannot guarantee suitability for every vehicle. Please rest assured that our Quality Control Manager will be reaching out to you shortly to personally review your case and provide further assistance, ensuring the matter is handled properly. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered a steering rack for my 2009 Audi A4 B8 2.0l TDI (143hp) 2 weeks ago. The delivery was fast, it arrived in only a few days, which i liked. Yet it turns out a part was missing in the delivery: The metal plug. Instead, it was delivered with a plastic one to keep the dust from entering. According to my local mechanic, without the right plug / lock, the plastic one wouldn't keep up with the oil pressure and it would just explode.I have tried to contact AUTODOC in multiple ways.-The AI assistant on their app told me I'd get an e-mail in 24-48 hours, 4 days later I haven't received anything.-I have called their numbers in hope of reaching an actual human instead of an AI, but guess what. Dan the virtual assistant picked up and he was no help at all. I could repeat a sentence 3 times and he wouldn't understand a thing I said, so after some time he gave up and said "We're happy we could solve your problem!" Next I wanted to see if it's possible to atleast return the steering rack, but no, "Unfortunately, this product is not eligible for a refund."I spent a total of 480 bucks on this thing, and for someone at the age of 19, I simply cannot afford this waste of money.And even if this so called "plug" isn't actually included in the product, then at least help me find the missing piece I need.I might not be experienced in cars and I could also be wrong about what I said in the beginning, it might just be called something else or it might just be an entirely other piece that I'm talking about. My main point is that there is literally no way for me to reach out to AUTODOC because of their awfully designed AI assistant.
supplier_response: Hello ! We’re very sorry to read about your experience and understand how frustrating this situation must be, especially given the importance and cost of the part. Regarding your case, we would like to clarify that we have attempted to contact you, however the phone number and email address provided with the order appear to be invalid, which prevents our team from reaching you directly. To properly investigate the missing metal plug, check the product specifications, and offer you the correct solution (replacement part, clarification, or return options), we kindly ask you to contact us directly by email at trust@autodoc.de. Please include clear photos of the received steering rack, and the area where the plug should be installed. Once we receive your email, our support team will review the case manually and assist you as quickly as possible. We truly regret the inconvenience and appreciate your patience. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: 2 out of t: I ordered a set of headlights for my Polo in May after someone smashed them backing into me. I had them fitted with my annual service in October (I am self-employed and didn’t wanna lose an extra day of work and income). Upon fitting the garage realises one of the lights is broken so I issue a warranty claim. They send me a replacement part very quickly- so far, so good.However, the part I was sent is for brakes. So, I send it back using their return label. It arrives on the 24th of Nov (I have proof of tracking). And I wait. But no light. All I get is emails that details are missing. At first it’s my address. Then they say the returned parts haven’t been received. Last week their customer service assures me the case will be escalated and prioritised. Today they email me that they are closing it. More assurances that the warranty department will take care of it. Nobody can tell me what details are missing. There is no contact for the warranty department or an email address to contact them. review_text: After posting this complaint, I was eventually contacted and reimbursed for the incompatible parts. However, after a few messages, the employee who had initially reached out to me appeared to stop responding, which forced me to contact the in-app support again. Only then was a warranty claim filed and my money refunded. While I am grateful for the resolution, I remain concerned about the way it was handled.The company referred to this outcome as a ‘gesture of goodwill.’ In reality, complying with consumer protection law is not goodwill—it is a legal obligation. What troubles me most is that, instead of straightforward assistance, I personally had to spend a great deal of time researching laws, studying specific legal articles, and presenting them to prove that I had the right to a refund.I appreciate the final resolution, but the process was unnecessarily time-consuming, frustrating, and placed the entire burden on me as the customer.Order number: 119912765Order date: 10.09.2024I am extremely disappointed with Autodoc’s customer service and their refusal to take responsibility for an incorrect part selection.All my previous orders for my Honda Accord 8 (VIN: JHMCU35809C203590) were always placed through Autodoc’s support team, never by myself. I always relied on their staff to provide the correct parts. However, in this case Autodoc now falsely claims that I ordered the parts on my own without their involvement, which is absolutely not true.The lower control arms that I received do not fit my vehicle. This is a clear case of “Sachmangel” under §434 BGB (German Civil Code) — the goods do not conform to the contract, as they are not suitable for the intended purpose. According to §439 BGB, in such cases the seller is obliged to provide a correct replacement or a full refund, and also to compensate the customer for costs of removal/installation.Instead, Autodoc is trying to hide behind a 14-day return period, which is irrelevant here. The 14-day return policy only applies to voluntary returns. When the goods are not as described or do not match the contract due to the seller’s mistake, the time limit does not apply — this is confirmed by EU consumer law (Directive 1999/44/EC and Directive (EU) 2019/771 on consumer sales and guarantees).In other words:	•	The parts were selected incorrectly by Autodoc staff (as in all my previous orders).	•	The parts do not fit — this is legally a non-conforming good.	•	Autodoc must provide a replacement or refund under BGB §§434–439 and EU Directive 2019/771.	•	Refusing support and shifting blame onto the customer is a breach of EU consumer rights.I am now forced to escalate this case to the European Consumer Centre (ECC-Net) and file a formal complaint. This whole experience has shown me that Autodoc’s customer support is unwilling to take responsibility when their own mistake causes problems for the buyer.⚠️ Buyers beware: if Autodoc makes a mistake in selecting your parts, they will try to deny responsibility and hide behind irrelevant “return policies.”
supplier_response: Hello Александр Пятковский! We regret you faced all the inconvenience. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered some parts from AUTODOC and when they arrived in the UK at the EVRI parcel delivery depo, the EVRI tracking system told me “This parcel is bigger than we expected, so a specialist Evri courier will need to deliver it.”   So it is coming up to 5 days that the parcel has been with EVRI, and I am still waiting to hear when my parcel is going to be delivered.   I contacted AUTODOC told them the parcel was oversized.   AUTODOC said “It is very unlikely that the package was oversized. All parts are inside normal package 750x200x950”.  I have confirmed with EVRI and their size limit is “Add together the 2 shortest sides and multiply it by 2. Then add the length. If the total is less than 245cm, your good to go”.   So doing this with the dimensions AUTODOC gave me for my parcel (750x200x950) the calculation would be 200 +  750 = 950.   Then 950 x 2 = 1900.  Then take the 1900 + 950 = 2850!   A total of 2850mm shows this parcel is over the EVRI size limit of 2450mm.   So it is oversized for EVRI based on AUTODOC’s own admissions.   EVRI also tells parcel senders “If your parcel is Overweight or Oversized then we’ll send the additional costs on to the sender and keep the parcel until we receive payment”.   So if AUTODOC don’t admit what they have done and look into what I have told them (will AUTODOC do this, because as far as AUTODOC are concerned the delivery is still not classified as late or there isn’t a size issue as far as they are concerned) so looking at other complaints on Trustpilot this could be weeks before this is sorted out.I have to also add this is the Second order in a row now, I have had a delivery issue with.  So I have 4 direct questions for AUTODOC which are as follows.  1)	Why do you send out oversized parcels to EVRI?   2)	Even if this was an AUTODOC accident sending it via EVRI, why does you customer service team not know about the size limit with EVRI and go immediately to resolve the problem?  3)	Is the issue I have having also happening to others?    4)	When am I going to get my parcel?   AUTODOC if you reply to this post, please give actual direct answers to all my questions and not some standard holding comment like “Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible.”  Thank you.  John
supplier_response: Hello John, We are sorry you faced inconveniences. We will check situation with the parcels' sizes. Our quality control manager will contact you in the nearest time. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

Few months ago I purchased a product on eBay from ATP Autotelie, now it broke because of a clear defect (it broke in two parts), and due to its breakage I was close to get my car engine completely burned. I had to spend more money to replace the defective product on an emergency basis, with a lot of time waste.Not even three months were passed, so the product was and still is under purchase warranty coverage, which is defined by the UE Council directive 85/374/CEE of 25th July 1985.However, ATP Autotelie has blocked all ways to contact them or rise any kind of issue.Therefore, I tried try to assert my customer rights through the PayPal claiming system, but the seller invented the excuse that somehow I “altered” the product, which is clearly false and can be easily verified from the picture I sent them. Anyway, PayPal decided to support the fraud and rejected my claim.The matter does not end here, I am reporting what happened (all of which can be proven with documentation in hand) first here on TrustPilot, and then I am turning to a consumer defense association to take legal action, not only against the seller but also against PayPal.This is not so much for an economic problem, but because people cannot be defrauded in this way, it is a question of principle and a question of law, both national and European community laws.**** Update ****After the review was published on TrustPilot, I was contacted by ATP Autoteile, unlocking the possibility to message them on eBay messaging system (but why it was blocked, nobody said).They behaved like they didn't know anything about the claim, that looks quite suspicious to me: did eBay arbitrarily take the initiative to reject my complaint without even informing the seller and without any reasoning? Let's take this as the truth, since the level of eBay service is so poor that can actually arrive at this point.After this, they formally apologized (formally) and accepted to refund the faulty product (except shipping costs), without wanting the broken product back. Then. we had days and days of endless email exchange to undergo the steps of the refund process, several their emails were also sent me twice from different email address. If I could count all the time I lost to reply and send all the information they asked, probably I had to receive three-four times the due amount, but probably this was exactly the idea under such behavior.Obviously I just received the refund of the faulty product (except shipping costs), that is already a huge step on towards the right direction, but the experience was so frustrating that I cannot avoid helping others to avoid my same mistake.

review_text: Your typical shady online retailer. Good prices, fast delivery as long as there's no problem, ordered quite a few things but never more. That's when they show their true morale. I'll edit this when I have the time, but in short: They will go through some lengths to save pennies (wrongly) and don't mind breaking written agreements to do so, even when they mess up in several ways on the same deliviery (deliberately ignoring cruicial emails, loosing the package, sending the wrong shock absorber packed in beat up/ducktaped box for the right one, promising free deliviery for all the mess but backing from that promise in the end when they once again mess up). When they see it's a never ending story (completely their own fault), they decided it'll probably cost to much to honor the agreement (despite me spending hundreds of euro for a lot of parts in the same order)  and just back away leaving me stranded witouth the most cruicial part, one out of four shock absorbers. The "customer service" is a joke, they have no feeling for right and wrong and will never go outside the manuscript, regardless if they messed up completely and put a customer in a seriously bad situation. Even the people in charge of customer service totaly lacks basic knowledge or just don't care.Will be posting the email conversations when I have the time so everyone can read it and weep. More than 70 emails back and forth over the course of almost 3 months to deal with the whole shitstorm with a bad ending (yeah, that's right) so will pack it up nicely.Stay away, trust me. Truly, truly horrible organisation.EDITReply to ATP:s reply: Yes, you refunded me the for the "second" shipping cost because you should have added that item in the forst order, you lost my order, it was greatly delayedand you sent me the wrong item in an opened and beat up box. So I have paid ONE shipping cost already according to our agreement, but still not recieved the item you agreed to send free of shipping.When you sent me the wrong shockabsorber in a opened and beat up box, which I sent back to you, you only refunded the shockabsorber and you refuse to send me a new one without me paying for the shipping cost again!Maybe it's easier to understand if I put it like this:1. First order. Lost, delayed and wrongly handled. Shipping paid.2. Second order. The item that should have been added to the first one (plus I added a bunch of other things=more money for you). Shipping cost paid again, and for the item of course.3. ONE shipping cost refunded according to agreement. (1 of 2 shipping costs paid)4. Wrong item recieved and returned.5. Wrong item refunded only. (Back at square one, with me missing an item that should have been sent in the first order.)6. You refuse to send a new one for some absurd reason, even though you have sent me the wrong one. Only reply is to place a new order and pay for shipping AGAIN.Do you still not understand?!

review_text: I’m pretty sure that this review makes no difference to ATP or my case, but hopefully people will think twice before buying anything from this company.I purchased a Sachs clutch kit from ATP, for my Audi A4. The payment and delivery went fine and quick, which is probably the only positive thing I have to say about this company.We had scheduled a repair at a local mechanic for a gearbox and clutch replacement, so we delivered the ATP clutch as per agreement to the mechanic. However, when the mechanic went to fit the new clutch to the car, he noticed that the clutch didn’t fit and was actually a LUK clutch (completely different brand to Sachs). The clutch was branded LUK on one side and Sachs on the other. As the car was taking up space on a lift and the mechanic couldn’t wait for a new clutch to be delivered, we had to buy the correct clutch locally. Unfortunately it didn’t come with a clutch release bearing, so we had to use the one supplied with the clutch kit from ATP.I called ATP and explained the situation (in German, as apparently only one or two ATP employees speaks English) and asked if it was possible for me to return the incorrect clutch without the bearing, to which they replied yes. I returned the clutch as agreed, and for a long period of time I heard nothing from them, so I decided to call them to see if they received the clutch. After a couple of calls, they informed me that they had in fact received the clutch, but that the bearing was missing and that they could not refund me as the set was not complete. I tried to explain the situation several times with no luck and no sympathy.ATP sells the exact clutch release bearing on their website and I informed them, that I would happily pay for the bearing that I had used from the kit, so they could replace it in the kit. But for some reason this is impossible to do for ATP. In theory I could just get the clutch back, buy the bearing, put it in the box and return a complete set. I have now spent several hours on the phone, trying to first of all get through to them on the phone, which is a nightmare. And when I finally manage to get through, nobody speaks English (fortunately I speak a little German). The employees that answer the phone cannot make any decisions and needs to speak to their supervisors and promises to call back, which they NEVER do, so you have to start all over every time you get through to them.To make things even better, now ATP wants me to pay them to return the wrong clutch to me.

Talking about: atp-autoteile.DEBe very careful! Bought a catalytic converter for a Renault, they did sent it to me but forgot the parts to assemble it. (They were included in the price and shown on the website.)  Because the bad quality it could not be fitted with the original Renault or other clamps. The flange was much to low. After a every email they promised me to send the missing parts within a few days. But they didn't. Tried to call them but could not reach them. (Tried it 17 times one afternoon, they put you on hold and then disconnect after 5-10 minutes waiting.)So brought the car to garage where they welded the catalytic converter to the exhaust system. To my surprise, after 10 weeks or more, I got without any warning the missing parts in the mail! Thanks!Real sad company, they don't even try to help their customers!

review_text: "no risk 90 day money back guarantee". Yeah sure.Bought a Chinese made A/C compressor for my Mercedes from them. It leaked badly. Not because it was broken or something. The design was bad and impossible to  create a sealed system.Sent the unit back. They made every single excuse you could imagine - I hadn't given them a chance to fix the problem, so no warranty (I did  they answered no questions about making the product actually work). - I didn't include the original mechanic's invoice - (I did, right on top of the compressor)- They inspected the thing and claimed  there was no proof of the defect occuring before the installation (simply, the replacement part was of a bad design - didn't work before, didn't work after, despite trying many things to prevent having to pay to uninstall and then install a different one)- The invoice didn't show what they needed to see - no warranty (my Italian speaking mechanic hardly understands what I ask him in english, however, I made sure he did the job right - flushing, replacing the drying unit, etc.)In short, they kept the part (probably re-sold it) and kept the money also!Edited October 25, 2018: ATP tried hard to remove this review about a year ago. Fortunately, after trustpilot looked at the evidence, this review remained. Don't be fooled by the feeble bluff of an offer to resolve the issue more than two years after the fact. Do yourself a favour and listen to my and all others' experience in buying cheap, poorly made non-functional parts and purchase your car parts from a reputable source. Otherwise, we told you so. My mistake cost me 179 euros for the compressor plus nearly 300 euros extra for my mechanic's huge efforts to try to make the thing work and then remove it and replace it with a unit from Mercedes.

review_text: Autodoc offers articles at a price, but charges you more at the checkout. It is not dynamic pricing, that is branding for fraudulent behaviour.Then, they email you 5 minutes to tell you that the part you ordered is not available, only a more expensive one, and continue to do the same for other parts on your paid order.When I finally cancelled the order, I was already on my third additional payment to replace parts not in stock. Note: They cannot update their site with live-time existing stock, but can immediately send you an email with updated, more expensive, offers of stock available!In the end they refund you the value advertised initially, not what you paid extra.They also keep your money in your (theirs) "active balance" rather than returning it.I am still waiting for some of those additional payments to be returned to my bank account. To work with them, and GLS, their couriers, is a task in itself where you're more likely to lose money and time than get on with your repairs.

review_text: Ordered part for vehicle. Site said it was suitable for vehicle. Part wrong size for vehicle. Contacted Autodoc and they said return it but at my cost. The cost of return would have been more than item, convenient for them to avoid any returns, shipping had been charged already to be supplied incorrect part. Wouldn't recommend this company as they make a mistake and you bear the cost.  Follow up from Autodoc to my complaint was to supply the correct part at no further cost but they say you have to supply original part number or vin number to confirm correct part selection. Seems that there system doesn't link vin to registration number which seems strange! As for supplying OEM numbers are not practical to do on some items as this means the vehicle would be unusable while waiting for parts, not really a reasonable thing to do for most people. I eventually received the correct part at no extra cost after customer care got involved. The customer care were very good and keen to keep me happy. I received a voucher as a good will gesture also. I still think they need to rethink their policy on   wrong parts supplied as they are the experts and when it says it fits your vehicle then it  doesn't, it should not cost you anything to return and to charge an extra premium on shipping terms to avoid this isn't acceptable. I have changed my rating to ok but will be careful of ordering anything that I need urgently. Again I was treated well by customer care.
supplier_response: Hello Bruce, We're incredibly sorry about your disappointment with our service. Please note that by registration number you can only limit the list of parts to parts that fit your car model. The final check for compatibility is possible only with the help of the OEN (Original Equipment Number). Before placing an order, you should check the OEN indicated in the description of the item. Also note, that we offer a SO (Safe Order) option at checkout which allows you to return within 200 days and gives you a free return label. Please contact us at help@autodoc.de and provide your order details, so we can check your case. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Poor dispatch times and customer support for this order. I have ordered with Autodoc many times before and in 3 different countries, the parts and packaging are very good and generally the order comes within 2 weeks. However on this order I have been very disappointed. The order was taken on the 29th of April and I checked all the parts were in stock before the order was made. Payment was taken out of my bank on Wednesday the 1st of May but it took until the evening of the 7th of May for the parts to be dispatched. I contacted Autodoc customer support today the 13th of May as I was concerned that the last entry on the Tracking from the courier was made at 7pm on the 7th of May. I asked the customer support agent if they could contact GLS on my behalf to confirm that they actually had the parcel, as when I contacted GLS this morning I was told that only the ticket had been produced and they did not have the parcel. All the customer support agent did was copy the link to the parcel tracking that said it was in Germany to me and said I would have to wait till tomorrow or Wednesday for the parcel tracking to be updated. I am now left to wait, hoping that the Parcel is going to come but not knowing whether GLS actually have it or not. When I contacted customer support I at least expected them to contact the delivery company to confirm that my Parcel was on route and not lost.EditThe Parcel arrived Today the 14th of May. It was as usual well Packaged, I just wish that when I contacted the customer service they could have checked the tracking for me so that I knew that the parcel wasn't lost in transit rather than just telling me to wait and see!

I was told that the parts were swapped for "STARK" parts because it would be quicker for me, adding that they would not do this to me in any future purchases. Having read several forums on this, it seems this is common practice with AUTODOC and they will exchange parts without notice. If it were a cheap like for like part then I would accept it no questions. This is not a sustainable business model, I hope AUTODOC will take this as constructive criticism and develop a form of integrity so as to respect and honour their customers choices and reasonable expectations. review_text: Upd.  10/18/2022Changed rating +1 star. Special thanks to Alex, who supported me from the German office (at his request, I am writing an update). The most interesting thing (I don't remember for sure ) is that this letter arrived just on the day when the goods were delivered to me.Despite this, I started a correspondence with support. The summary of the correspondence: thanks for the coupon, but I'm not going to order from you anymore, because your service is s h i t. At some point, an employee of the quality department from Germany joined in, who tried to smooth the situation.Through a series of correspondence with Alex (support from German), the value of the coupon was increased from 3 euros to 20 euros and with Alex's personal assistance in the following purchases. He also explained the reason for the delay. I, in turn, explained that I, as a client, I'm not interested in their problems. We agreed that the company will work on its mistakes.IMHO: - regarding the autodoc: let's see if I do order something. I will look at other competitors- my personal: no matter what country you are in, but if you don’t stand up for your rights and demand the best quality that you pay for, you will use s h i t t y service all the time we all should work on our improvements Original comment Placed an order and paid for it on Monday (September 05) after 14:00. Today (September 13) the status is "Order is awaiting receipt by the carrier". That is, 1 working week has passed from the moment of the order to the moment of its transfer to the postal service. I am silent about how long the delivery itself will take place.In fact, starting from the 3rd day, my order changed its status every day (it’s a pity that the client has no way to track this in the system). There was nothing unique in the order, at the time of ordering and payment all the goods were in stock, no one contacted me to say that there were difficulties and the formation of the order would take a whole week.At the same time, the website indicates that the order will be sent within 24-48 hours.Honestly, I do not understand how this is possible today for a large integrator when we all strive to speed up the delivery of goods/services to customers.  And I understand that I don’t have much choice but to go to some local store and buy what I need.I recently moved to Portugal, but in this service, I went back 10-15 years in the past. Monday morning, I log in to your site at best, I place the order for the offered items that you have offered on the site, with a note that they are available immediately, that is, from the warehouse.I pay, not with credit card, rather than choosing a payment immediately, Sofortüberweisung, believing in honest salesmen intention, pkwteile.deFrom the site PKWteile.deSofortüberweisung:Sofortüberweisung ist das Direct-Überweisungsverfahren der Payment Network AG. What's Sofortüberweisung stellen Sie noch während Ihrer Bestellung bequem eine Überweisung über den jeweiligen Betrag in Ihr Online-Banking-Konto ein. Wir erhalten nach der Abschluss der Sofortüberweisung eine Echtzeitbestätigung. I go to the bank account and really, The billing company took the amount in €, how much the order price was ordered, plus the delivery costs, which is implied.Okay, that is, with the experience of buying, of course not on that page, but with some others, I conclude that the order is in preparation and that parts will arrive in a reasonable time.At the end of the week, five days have passed, I go to the pkw teile internet store page to see what's going on with my paid order.I sign up to the page, and I see the order still standing in my account, as if it was not paid, and waiting for some confirmation. It's Saturday 19.05.2018. I think it's a weekend, okay to look after the holidays.22.05.2018 I open the page again, no changes in my account and my € are already in the company's account. I do not really care about the terms of payment, I just want my ordered parts. ( i am still waiting, 28,05,2018,) In case we refuse your order we will inform you immediately via email.After that, I investigate which e mail  I would be able to regret to not deliver my order and find it at infopkwteile.de.At this address I send a brief note of the title: "Wo sind meine ersatzteile?" expecting some feedback in a reasonable time.Disappointed for everything I've read, I come to the conclusion that I have lost my paid and paid order, and thus the money I earned is out of the pkw teile.de site.Everything I planned, I could not accomplish, and i planed 20 days in advance.However, this business will not just leave forgiveness, it just begins.Today, 28,05,2018, on my pkwteile.de account, nothing happened, for them everything is cool.-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------29,05,2018,Thank You for the fast response, Where does this answer say that you do not have any products at the warehouse and offer other products that are more expensive than those you've presented on your page as available? review_text: Ordered several parts, one of them being a rear muffler of an old Polo. This thing is maybe 1m long. Ordered on 27/5, today, 6/6 it still shows it's somewhere in transit in Germany. However, I had to pay extra €50(!!!) because it's "oversized". Customer online help is useless, repeating the same automated line that it's oversized and it will arrive, eventually. I ordered much bigger things in the past like an engine crane, never had to pay any "oversided" fee and then wait 2 weeks for it to arrive. Useless and not to use if you need the parts quickly. Oh, eventhough I paid autodoc, now it's not their problem and I have to discuss it further with the shipping company NOX. Easy peasy, right, it's my problem now? Oh yeah, as my packages are still somewhere in Germany 6 days on the same place, without any updates, it's worth mentionaing that autodoc uses a shipping company which has 0(!!) positive reviews, just 12 1-star complaints. As to the answer below, I'm still waitingUpdate: 10/6 contacted support via chat (only option). They see that the packages didn't move for almost a week now. They will contact NOX to verify the situation. When will I get the answer? They don't know this, I have to wait. They don't even know themselves how to get information from a shipping company they use and for which they charge in total almost €75 to deliver a small muffler. Update 14/6: I didn't even hear once from the customer service and ordered parts still in the same place somewhere in Germany. Nox.express doing Nox things I guess. Meanwhile my car is still off the road. I guess I will have to rent one and bill autodoc. If they would put half the effort they're putting in trying to get my reviews removed, into solving the issue, it would maybe progress things.

German is not my mother tongue so I am writing in English.I have ordered twice at kfzteile24.de and twice they managed to make mistakes. The first time they sent a wrong part. It was labelled wrong in their stock, mistakes happen and I could return the part at no cost (international shipment!), so I won't blame them at that point. But then I got the very same, wrong part again! They didn't learn from their mistake... So I had to send back again (at their expense), and had to wait again for the replacement. Finally I got the correct part. But it took a lot of e-mails with their customer support and four weeks altogether... Last week I ordered again, a fairly large amount of parts. They sent me an e-mail that one part wasn't in stock, so they offered to send another brand (more expensive) at the same cost. Hooray for their service, I really appreciated it. But what they actually sent me, was an opened box with a used part! It got scratches and dirt on it! As it was still useable and had cost me only 10 Euro (normal price in local stores 25 Euro so still a bargain) I decided to use the part anyway and sent them an angry e-mail instead. Which they never answered... A friend of mine also had bad experience with kfzteile24, ordered a cilinder head gasket for an 8V cilinder head and got one for a 16V cilinder head instead. He had to talk endlessly to their customer support to get *their* fault rectified. I will hesistate to do business with them again. Pity, because if they didn't f*ck up every time, they would get five stars as they deliver quality branded parts for very reasonable prices.

review_text: I have ordered 2 Bilstein air struts for Mercedes and the first thing was that the price on the website was 695€ per piece but when I confirmed the order the price was suddenly 712€ per piece. Then first few days the status of the order didn't change at all, then it was that items are in a transfer from one warehouse to another and then finally that the items are waiting for shipping company to pick them up. And then it starts... After 2 weeks! the status was still the same so I contacted them. Next day they called me that those parts are not available and asked if they can send me  some replacement from different producer. When I asked them how is this even possible regarding the fact that exactly the same parts are still available on their website with the info "In Stock" they replied that the parts are in another warehouse so if I want those I need to wait additional few days. And I waited another 4 days and then I got a call that they can send me those parts but now the price is almost 1000€ higher!!!!! So at the end they wanted almost double the price for the parts which were already payed and confirmed 3 weeks ago (and status of my order was already "warten auf abholung durch transportfirma")!!! When I didn't agree to pay additional 1000€ they simply said that they will cancel my order and make a refund. This is really unacceptable and unprofessional or you can call it a scam!Never ever again!!!EDIT: I sent an email as suggested in reply below. supplier_response: Sehr geehrter Herr Schneider, vielen Dank für Ihre Mitteilung. 1. Eine Suche nur mit Fahrzeugauswahl oder anhand KBA-Nummer nicht immer ausreichend, da nur die FIN die Ausstattung sowie alle individuelle Besonderheiten einzelnes Autos berücksichtigt. Aufgrund OEM-Nummern kann man auch eine ausreichende Auswahl durchzuführen da diese Nummern zur direkten Übereinstimmung zwischen der gleichen Teile verschiedener Hersteller führen. Beachten Sie bitte auch was auf der Warenkarte steht: " Bitte beachten Sie, dass die abschließende Überprüfung der Kompatibilität nur mit Hilfe der sogenannten OEN ( Original Equipment Number) möglich ist. Die Beschreibung unserer Produkte enthalten nur technische Spezifikationen und können deren Kompatibilität mit einem bestimmten Fahrzeug nicht garantieren. Bevor Sie  eine Bestellung aufgeben , sollen Sie die in der Beschreibung des entsprechenden Artikels angegebene OEN überprüfen". 2. Sie haben beim Auftrag keine Option Sicher Kaufen gewählt, daher trägt der Kunde Rückversandkosten. Wir haben Ihnen unsere Anfrage per E-Mail gesendet und warten auf Ihre Antwort. Beachten Sie bitte dass Sie uns jederzeit per: trust@autoteiledirekt.de erreichen. Mit freundlichen Grüßen, Ihr Autoteiledirekt Team

This review is for the company Autodoc or autoteiledirekt.de (they seem to operate under a bunch of different names, so I posted here and on Autodoc reviews).The short version: Absolutely terrible customer service, bordering on a scam. Order somewhere else!The very long one for those who care:I ordered a bunch of parts from them and to be fair the items are what I expected and do their jobs but 2 days after the items were supposed to be at my house (shipped inside Germany so no excuse there) I called them up and after pretty unfriendly interaction with their customer service guy I got promised that it would go out that day. So, if it says 2 days, expect a week.While I was talking to him I noticed the parts where now 30 Euros cheaper on a 200 Euro order and I asked him for a deal. I would have taken store credit, even a complimentary sponge or a packet of gummy bears or something that showed any interest in their customers, but he wasn't willing to budge. Fair enough there too, they don't have to. So, I told him I would just order the stuff again and return the first package and since they already delivered the parts too late for me I had all the time in the world (second package was late by the same time frame too). I even bought their safe order (sicher bestellen) feature on the first order, which states that I can return the uninstalled parts for a full refund for any reason within a 100 days or something like that. Since the boxes are just sitting here and haven’t even been opened it shouldn't have been a problem. A week after I filed for the return I haven't even gotten a mail saying that it is being processed other than their automated instant reply so I called them up again. I didn’t even get an answer the first 4 times I called even though it kept telling me constantly I was next in line. After 15 Minutes and 27 Seconds you get dropped from the line each time so you get to try again. I have a headset and plenty of time while i was doing other things so I just kept calling. Even tried calling them from 3 lines at once (I got bored) and you get the same message on all of them. That was around 10am on a regular business day. Never got through to anyone then so I tried again in the afternoon and after the second round of waiting I actually got someone who couldn't even say hello or good bye or anything and I tried my best to be extra friendly. I got some results in the form of six emails (one for each part I ordered) saying my return was accepted and an attached PDF Return Form for every part. Seemed like progress but it also said that my return mailers would come in separate emails which still haven't arrived 5 days later.I guess I will listening to some more annoying music and automated messages next week. At this point I fully expect them to either not answer at all or at best be extremely rude again. Chances of me getting the full refund, that their extra safe order service promises are slim to none and I bet they will give me the full runaround for that too.Calling it what it is at this point is a Scam.I will change this review accordingly if they manage to surprise me and not do any of that, after all terrible service and late deliveries aren't a scam if the parts are what you bought but at this point it sure looks like a Scam when it comes to their return policies and safe order options.Update (26.06.2018):I guess complaining loudly on the internet does help since their english speaking customer service called me up first thing monday and sent me the return mailer (just one though even though I got two boxes but since they already had all the heavy stuff in one box and the 2 super light things in a seperate one I figured they might as well go in a single box. Didn't want to argue over this but I took a bunch of pictures anyway). The english speaking customer service rep was very nice, which was a welcome change so I guess I'll be carefully optimistic and wait.Update (09.07.2018):Turns out they actually did send me three labels - in yet again three separate emails - just two ended up in spam. I guess since it took 40something emails (not even kidding) to get to this point. So I sent the three packages in the same arrangement they came here and apparently they received them on Jul. 2nd. Still waiting to hear anything further. Don't hold your breath on getting your money in any sort of timely fashion back. Mind you I started the Process over a month ago on June 3rd. I do realize the return proves and banks and all that take their sweet time too. I don't have a problem with that, but this is just ridiculous. How does a company with that terrible of a customer service stay in business these days? I've head better customer service from Indian tech support scammers than these guys. I’m curious what happens next. My guess is I do get some money back at some point or another but not even close to the amount I paid with all kinds of bogus charges and “service” fees as a justification. I guess we will see.

Please print the return mark on an A4 paper, separate the return label at the marked point and stick it on your return. review_text: Deutsche Übersetzung weiter unten._______________________________It took 8 days to answer my matter about a product that did not fit!I ordered a lock cylinder (with two keys) with the correct OE number and to fit my car (also according to VIN code/ chassis number and registration number, but in the box was a completely wrong model. The support blames me that did not look up properly before purchase and that I am responsible for shipping (which costs more than the product is worth). Has sent pictures of comparisons between the products, but the support does not care and does not look up with the manufacturer. I do not know which cars it fits and therefore can not sell it, so I'll scrape it.Order from Autoteiledirekt if you think you will not have any problems with the products, but otherwise avoid! ……….................................................Update! I have finally received free shipping back for the lock cylinder, but I think Autoteiledirekt has taken a long time to realize the mistake that is mainly the manufacturer's.It should be added that the number on the product itself is correct._______________________________Es dauerte 8 Tage, um meine Frage zu einem Produkt zu beantworten, das nicht passte!Ich bestellte einen Schließzylinder (mit zwei Schlüsseln) mit der richtigen OE-Nummer und für mein Auto (auch nach FIN/ Fahrgestellnummer und Zulassungsnummer, aber in der Box war ein völlig falsches Modell. Die Unterstützung beschuldigt mich, dass ich vor dem Kauf nicht richtig nachgesehen habe und dass ich für den Versand verantwortlich bin (was mehr kostet als das Produkt wert ist). Hat Bilder von Vergleichen zwischen den Produkten gesendet, aber die Unterstützung ist egal und nicht beim Hersteller nachschlagen. Ich weiß nicht, welche Autos es passt und kann es daher nicht verkaufen, also werde ich es kratzen.Bestellen Sie beim Autoteiledirekt, wenn Sie der Meinung sind, dass Sie keine Probleme mit den Produkten haben, aber vermeiden Sie dies!……….................................................Aktualisierung!Ich habe endlich kostenlosen Versand für den Schließzylinder bekommen, aber ich denke, dass Autoteiledirekt lange gedauert hat, um den Fehler zu erkennen, der in erster Linie der Hersteller ist.Es sollte hinzugefügt werden, dass die Nummer des Produkts selbst korrekt ist.
